name: Just Coffee Cooperative
notes: Worker-owned roaster Madison WI since 2001. Fair trade organic. B-Corp certified. Sources from 15+ farmer co-ops worldwide.

name: Cooperative Coffee Roasters
notes: Asheville NC worker co-op. Roasts to order Mon–Thu, ships nationally. Single origins and blends. Subscription club available.

name: Thread Coffee Roasters
notes: Baltimore MD worker co-op since 2012. 2025 Roast Magazine Roaster of the Year finalist. B-Corp certified. Online store with subscriptions.

name: Gimme Coffee Cooperative
notes: Ithaca NY worker co-op, one of the first worker-owned coffee companies in the US. Founded 2000. Online store with subscriptions.

name: Afterglow Coffee Cooperative
notes: Richmond VA worker co-op since 2021. Former Lamplighter employees. Ships Mon–Thu. Online shop at afterglowcoffee.com/shop.

name: Morning Bell Coffee Roasters
notes: Iowa's first worker co-op, Ames IA since 2022. Cafe and roaster. Wholesale coffee available. Verify online national shipping.

name: Rock City Coffee
notes: Rockland/Rockport Maine worker co-op since 1999. Ships anywhere in the US. Free local delivery in mid-coast Maine.

name: Slow Bloom Coffee Cooperative
notes: Redlands CA worker co-op formed by workers illegally laid off by Augie's Coffee. Active online shop with single origin coffees and subscriptions.

name: Equal Exchange
category: coffee, tea, chocolate, olive oil, nuts, snacks
notes: Pioneer worker co-op since 1986. Full online store. Coffee, tea, chocolate, olive oil, nuts, dried fruit. Free shipping over threshold.

name: Red Emma's
notes: Worker-owned radical bookstore, cafe and community space in Baltimore MD since 2004. Online bookshop. Strong politics, history, social theory selection.

name: Bluestockings Cooperative
notes: NYC Lower East Side queer feminist bookstore founded 1999, worker-owned since 2021. Online shop via bookshop.org.

name: Raven Book Store
notes: Lawrence KS. Employee-owned since 2022. 49% worker-owned. General interest bookstore with online ordering.

name: AK Press
notes: Long-running worker co-op publisher and distributor of radical books since 1990. Ships globally. Largest anarchist publisher in the English-speaking world.

name: Making Worlds Bookstore
notes: West Philadelphia nonprofit worker co-op bookstore. Online store available. Abolition and liberation focused.

name: Microcosm Publishing
notes: Portland OR worker-ownership program since 1996. Publishes and distributes DIY, punk, social justice zines and books. Also sells patches, stickers, buttons, shirts. US-printed.

name: Adobe Books & Arts Cooperative
notes: San Francisco Mission District volunteer worker co-op since 2013. Beloved community institution. Bookshop.org storefront: bookshop.org/shop/adobebooks.

name: Equal Exchange — Food
category: coffee, tea, chocolate, olive oil, nuts, snacks
notes: Full pantry range: coffee, chocolate, tea, olive oil, nuts, bananas available online. Worker co-op since 1986.

name: Real Pickles
ships: Northeast US only (New England + NY, NJ, PA)
notes: Greenfield MA worker co-op. Organic pickles, sauerkraut, kimchi, hot sauce. Direct online store with bulk pricing. Northeast distribution only.

name: Once Again Nut Butter
notes: Nunda NY ESOP. Organic peanut, almond, cashew, sunflower butters. Widely available online and in stores.

name: Tootie's Tempeh
ships: in-store only (ME to NJ region, Whole Foods New England)
notes: Biddeford ME worker co-op. Plastic-free fermentation. No direct online sales — perishable product. Find at Whole Foods New England and natural food stores.

name: Democracy Brewing
notes: 100% worker-owned brewery and pub in Boston MA since 2018. 'Fermenting rebellion.' Craft beer, scratch-made food. Has online shop — check site for current offerings.

name: Drifa Brewing Company
notes: One of nine cooperative breweries in the US, the only one in Michigan. Taproom and beer garden in Marquette MI. Worker co-op since founding.

name: Palestinian Soap Cooperative
notes: Palestinian and American member-owned co-op founded 2021. Traditional Nablus olive oil soap. Ships worldwide from Palestine and US warehouses. 8,000+ reviews.

name: Soapbox Co-op
notes: Chicago worker co-op founded 2025. Concentrated soap drops, all natural organic ingredients. Profits split equally among workers. Online store + Chicago farmers markets.

name: TESA Collective
notes: Worker-owned co-op making social justice board games. Titles: Co-opoly, Rise Up, STRIKE!, Space Cats Fight Fascism. Made in USA with union/co-op printers.

name: Justseeds Artists' Cooperative
notes: Worker-owned co-op of 40 North American printmakers since 1998. Social/political/environmental themed prints and posters. Active online store in Pittsburgh.

name: Microcosm Publishing — Zines & Art
notes: Also sells patches, stickers, enamel pins, posters, buttons, shirts beyond books and zines.

name: Subvert
notes: Cooperatively-owned Bandcamp alternative. 19,000+ artists, 2,800+ labels across 120+ countries. Artists keep 100% of earnings. Live as of 2025.

name: Mirlo
notes: Open-source worker co-op music platform. Artists set their own fees. Already live and functional. Fairer Bandcamp alternative.

name: Means Workwear
notes: Minneapolis worker co-op. USA hand-printed tees, hoodies, hats with labor movement and political themes. Direct to consumer, active store.

name: Worx Printing
notes: Worker-owned union co-op in Worcester MA. Custom and retail tees, hats, bags. United Steelworkers affiliated.

name: Maggie's Organics
notes: Oldest organic apparel company in the US, founded 1992. Organic cotton socks, tights, leggings, and apparel. GOTS certified. Production via worker co-ops in Nicaragua and NC.

name: No Gods No Masters
notes: Collectively-managed co-op of artists. Political and activist tees, sweatshop-free, vegan inks. Donates portion of sales to activist causes.

name: Create the Collection
notes: Consumer storefront for clothing made by Opportunity Threads worker co-op in NC.

name: Custom Collaborative / Fashion That Works
notes: NYC worker co-op of immigrant and low-income women. Made-to-measure and small batch. Contact directly to purchase.

name: Blue Tin Production
notes: Chicago co-op run by refugee and immigrant women. Primarily B2B manufacturer for designers. Contact for consumer orders.

name: Artisans Cooperative
category: handmade marketplace — ceramics, textiles, home decor, art, jewelry
notes: Member-owned Etsy alternative launched 2023. Hundreds of sellers offering handmade home goods, ceramics, candles, textiles. Best single marketplace source for co-op-made goods.

name: Wood Shop Workers Co-op
notes: Vancouver BC worker co-op. Custom reclaimed wood furniture and millwork for homes and businesses. Founded 2014.

name: Handwork — Ithaca's Artisan Cooperative
notes: Founded 1976. Worker-owned shop in Ithaca NY selling handcrafted goods from local and regional makers. One of the oldest artisan co-ops in the US.

name: Opportunity Threads
notes: Worker-owned cut-and-sew plant in Morganton NC. Primarily B2B manufacturer. Immigrant-led co-op. Consumer sales through Create the Collection.

name: Breadfruit Cooperative
status: verify current status and online availability
notes: NYC-based cooperative focused on woodworking and Indigenous maker practices. Refurbished furniture and home goods for BIPOC communities.

name: Justseeds Artists' Cooperative — Prints
notes: 40 artists across US, Canada, Mexico. Affordable political printmaking. Ships from Pittsburgh PA.

name: TESA Collective — Games
notes: Co-opoly: learn cooperative economics. Rise Up: build a social movement. STRIKE!: fight a corporation. Space Cats Fight Fascism. All US-made.

name: REI Co-op
notes: Largest consumer co-op in the US. Outdoor apparel, gear. NOTE: consumer co-op (members are customers), not worker co-op.

name: Subvert — Music
notes: Collectively-owned Bandcamp successor. 0% platform fees. Artists keep 100% of earnings. Members vote on platform governance.

name: Mirlo — Music
notes: Open-source worker co-op. Artists set their own fee percentage. Already live with full functionality.

name: AK Press — Books
notes: Oakland CA worker co-op since 1990. Publishes and distributes radical, anarchist, and leftist books. Ships worldwide.

name: Soapbox Co-op — Soap
notes: Very new (2025), Chicago. Concentrated soap drops — fill your own dispenser, pay only for soap not water. All natural organic ingredients.

name: Palestinian Soap Cooperative — Soap
notes: Member-owned 2021. Nablus traditional hot-process olive oil soap. Warehouses in Palestine and USA. 8,000+ reviews.

name: Equal Exchange — Tea
notes: Fair trade organic teas sourced from farmer co-ops in India, Sri Lanka, South Africa. 20+ flavors. Part of broader Equal Exchange online store.

name: Equal Exchange — Chocolate
notes: Organic fair trade chocolate bars sourced from farmer co-ops. Part of broader Equal Exchange online store.

name: Red Emma's — Bookstore
notes: Online storefront via Bookshop.org. Ships nationally. Alternative URL if main site down.

name: Bluestockings — Bookstore
notes: Bookshop.org storefront for national ordering. NYC Lower East Side.

name: Making Worlds — Bookstore
notes: Bookshop.org storefront. West Philadelphia. Abolition, autonomy, Black/Brown/Indigenous liberation focus.

name: Frontier Co-op
notes: Member-owned co-op since 1976. Herbs, spices, teas, seasonings. NOTE: member co-op (stores are members), not worker co-op — flag distinction on site.

name: Once Again Nut Butter — Food
notes: 100% employee-owned ESOP. Organic peanut butter, almond, cashew, tahini, sunflower butters. One of the most worker-democratic ESOPs in the food industry.

name: Raven Book Store — Online
notes: Lawrence KS. Employee-owned 2022. Known for How to Resist Amazon and Why author Danny Caine. Online ordering available.

name: Afterglow Coffee — Shop
notes: Direct link to Afterglow shop. Single origin coffees roasted in Richmond VA. Ships nationally.

name: Thread Coffee — Store
notes: Direct link to Thread Coffee store. Subscriptions available. Also sells brewing equipment (Fellow, Chemex, Hario).

name: Microcosm Publishing — Books
notes: Publishers Weekly Fastest Growing Publisher 2022-2024. DIY, punk, witchcraft, bicycling, gender, self-care, social justice. US-printed.

name: Worx Printing — Shop
notes: Direct retail shop for union-made tees, hats, bags. Hosted on Good Press platform. Exclusive supplier of union labels for UNITE HERE and Workers United.

name: Real Pickles — Online Store
ships: Northeast US only (New England + NY, NJ, PA)
notes: Direct link to Real Pickles online store. Pack of 4 jars, cases of 12, bulk 1 gallon and 5 gallon buckets available. Northeast only.

name: Justseeds — Shop
notes: Direct link to Justseeds online shop. Affordable prints from 40 activist artists. Pittsburgh shipping hub.

name: TESA Collective — Store
notes: Full game store. Also sells shirts and activist merchandise. Free shipping available.

name: Artisans Cooperative — Marketplace
notes: Launched Oct 2023. Sellers pay no fees until profitable. Members govern platform democratically. Covers ceramics, jewelry, textiles, home goods, clothing.

name: Gimme Coffee — Online
notes: Ithaca NY, founded 2000. Worker co-op since recently. Multiple cafes in Ithaca. Online store with subscriptions.

name: Rock City Coffee — Online
notes: Rockland Maine worker co-op since 1999. Ships anywhere US. Free local delivery in mid-coast Maine.

name: Equal Exchange — Olive Oil & Nuts
notes: Fair trade organic olive oil and mixed nuts. Sourced from farmer co-ops in Palestine and elsewhere.

name: Slow Bloom — Coffee Shop
notes: Direct link to Slow Bloom coffee shop. Single origin coffees, subscriptions available. Founded by workers illegally fired during unionization.

name: Morning Bell — Coffee
notes: Iowa's first worker co-op business (2022). Site active. Wholesale and retail. Espresso bar and roastery in Ames IA.

name: Means Workwear — Apparel
notes: Worker co-op fighting for a worker-owned world. USA-produced, hand-printed. Labor movement themes, anti-fascist designs.

name: Maggie's Organics — Clothing
notes: Oldest organic apparel company in US. Employee-owned. Socks, tights, leggings, tops. Production at worker co-ops in Nicaragua and NC. GOTS certified.

name: No Gods No Masters — Apparel
notes: Artist collective cooperative. Sweatshop-free, vegan inks, fair labor certified. Political and activist designs. Ships worldwide.

name: Palestinian Soap Cooperative — Kufiya
notes: Also sells traditional kufiyas and Palestinian goods beyond soap. Palestinian and American co-owned.

name: Subvert — Platform Co-op
notes: 19,823 artists, 2,856 labels, 2,532 supporters across 120+ countries. 0% platform fees. Artists and fans co-own the platform.

name: Mirlo — Music Platform
notes: Open source. Artists set their own platform fee. No waiting lists. Full catalog and label profiles. The most functional Bandcamp alternative.

name: Soapbox Co-op — Chicago
notes: 2025 startup. Eco-friendly concentrated liquid soap. Workers split all profits equally. No investors, no managers.

name: Defector
notes: Worker-owned sports and culture site launched in 2020 by the former Deadspin staff who walked out together. Reader-supported, no outside investors, workers own 100%.

name: 404 Media
notes: Worker-owned investigative tech and cybersecurity outlet founded 2023 by four journalists. Exposes hacking, privacy vulnerabilities, and digital exploitation. Reader-supported.

name: Aftermath
notes: Reader-funded worker-owned video game and internet culture site keeping the spirit of old-school independent games journalism alive. Founded by veteran games journalists.

name: Hell Gate
notes: Sharp, fiercely independent local news outlet covering New York City politics and neighborhood culture. Worker-owned, reader-supported, unapologetically NYC.

name: Flaming Hydra
notes: Collaborative daily cooperative newsletter delivering unmediated essays, criticism, and art from over 65 creators. Each contributor owns an equal share.

name: Hearing Things
notes: Worker-owned music journalism platform built by veteran editors to critique and celebrate music without corporate oversight. Reader-supported.

name: Racket MN
notes: Subscriber-backed alternative source for local arts, news, and food culture across the Minneapolis–St. Paul Twin Cities. Worker-owned, independent.

name: Maximum Fun
notes: Pioneering listener-supported independent podcast network that converted to employee ownership in 2023. Home to My Brother, My Brother and Me; Judge John Hodgman; and dozens more.

name: The Colorado Sun
notes: Public-benefit, journalist-owned newsroom delivering rigorous investigative and statewide reporting across Colorado. Member-supported, founded 2018 by Denver Post journalists.

name: Rascal News
notes: Reader-supported, worker-owned journalism outlet dedicated exclusively to covering the tabletop RPG and board game industry. The only co-op in the games press.

name: The Grain Shed
notes: Worker and farmer-owned cooperative brewery and bakery in Spokane WA. Uses regionally grown grain. Taproom open Wed–Sun.

name: Bathtub Row Brewing Co-op
notes: Cooperative brewery in Los Alamos NM with both worker and consumer members. Named for historic Manhattan Project bathtubs repurposed as brewing tanks.

name: Fifth Street Brewpub
notes: Ohio's first cooperative brewpub in Dayton. Community-owned with member shares. On-site brewing, full food menu.

name: New Barons Brewing Cooperative
notes: Wisconsin's first cooperative brewery in Milwaukee. ~650 member-owners at $200/share. Operates as a brewery incubator helping new brewers launch.

name: Soft Star Shoes
notes: Worker-owned co-op in Corvallis, OR. Handcrafted minimalist leather shoes, moccasins, and boots for adults and kids. Made in USA.

name: Thorogood USA
notes: Made by Weinbrenner Shoe Company, employee-owned (ESOP). USA-made work boots including fire/logger/safety boots. Manufacturing in Merrill, WI since 1892.

name: Alvarado Street Bakery
notes: Worker co-op since 1981 in Petaluma, CA. Organic sprouted grain breads, tortillas, and bagels. Sold nationwide at grocery stores and direct online.

name: Artisan Beverage Cooperative
notes: Worker co-op in Greenfield, MA. Makes Katalyst Kombucha, Ginger Libation ginger beer, and Draft Mead. Sold regionally and online.

name: Cabot Creamery
notes: Farmer-owned cooperative (part of Agri-Mark) representing 800+ farm families across New England and NY. First dairy co-op to earn B Corp certification (2012). Cheddar, butter, yogurt, sour cream.

name: Tillamook
notes: Farmer-owned cooperative of ~80 farm families in Tillamook, OR. B Corp certified 2020. Cheese, ice cream, butter, sour cream, yogurt. Major national brand.

name: Cafe Campesino
notes: Fair trade organic coffee roaster in Americus, GA. Co-founded Cooperative Coffees importing co-op. Sources directly from farmer co-ops in Latin America and Africa. B Corp certified.

name: Bainbridge Vineyards
notes: Estate winery on a cooperative farm on Bainbridge Island, WA. First B Corp winery in Washington state. Organic estate-grown wines, direct-to-consumer and regional retail.

name: Means TV
notes: Worker-owned cooperative streaming service. $7.99/month. Films, documentaries, scripted shows, daily news. Available on Roku, Fire TV, Apple TV.

name: RADIX
notes: Black-led, worker-owned, union print shop and publisher in Brooklyn, NY. Publishes titles from marginalized community voices. Online bookstore at radix.coop/shop.

name: Catalytic Sound
notes: Artist-run cooperative of 33 musicians in Chicago. Free jazz and improvised music. Vinyl, CD, cassette, digital. 50% of sales go directly to musicians. Also offers Soundstream subscription.

name: TableTop Gaming
notes: Worker co-op game shop in Syracuse, NY (founded 2022). Magic: The Gathering, Pokémon, D&D, Pathfinder, Lorcana, board games, accessories. Starts pricing at 10% off MSRP. Free shipping over $125.

name: Fair State Brewing Cooperative
notes: One of the first cooperative breweries in the US, Minneapolis MN. Consumer-owned co-op — members elect the board. Online shop sells merch, memberships, and gifts.

name: Divine Chocolate
notes: Premium Fairtrade chocolate. 20% owned by Kuapa Kokoo, a Ghanaian farmer cooperative of 100,000+ cacao farmers. B Corp certified (score 114.9). Widely available online and in grocery stores.

name: Broken Clock Brewing Cooperative
notes: Minneapolis brewery where workers and community members can become owner-members. Taproom in NE Minneapolis with rotating craft beers.

name: Bob's Red Mill
notes: 100% employee-owned since 2010 when founder Bob Moore gifted the company to his workers on his 81st birthday. Whole grains, stone-ground flours, oats, baking mixes, and cereals.

name: King Arthur Baking Company
notes: 100% employee-owned since 2004. Vermont-based company selling premium flours, baking mixes, tools, and ingredients. Also a B Corporation.

name: Southern Exposure Seed Exchange
notes: Worker-owned cooperative with equal pay for all worker-owners. Specializes in heirloom and open-pollinated seeds adapted to the Mid-Atlantic region. Over 800 varieties.

name: Golden Artist Colors
notes: 100% employee-owned since 2021. Manufactures professional-grade acrylic and oil paints, mediums, and varnishes. Over 200 workers across three upstate New York facilities.

name: W. W. Norton & Company
notes: Oldest and largest employee-owned book publisher in the US, wholly owned by employees since the early 1960s. Known for the Norton Anthologies and thousands of trade and academic titles.

name: Grass Roots Farmers' Cooperative
notes: Farmer-owned cooperative in Pearcy, AR. Sells grass-fed beef, pastured pork, and pasture-raised poultry direct to consumers. Founded by small family farms committed to regenerative agriculture.

name: Dansko
notes: 100% employee-owned (ESOP) since 2012. West Grove, PA. Known for professional clogs and comfort footwear. Worn by nurses, chefs, and teachers. Danish-inspired design.

name: Left Hand Brewing Company
notes: 100% employee-owned (ESOP) since 2020. Longmont, CO. Known for Milk Stout Nitro. Online shop sells hats, shirts, glassware, and accessories.

name: American Roots
notes: Union-made apparel manufactured in Maine by USW Local 366 members. Hoodies, tees, and labor movement gear. Workers are represented by United Steelworkers. 'Made in USA, Union Strong.'

name: 1620 Workwear
notes: Massachusetts-based brand that completely owns its domestic garment factories. Premium workwear, pants, and field gear made in USA with fair wages and ethical supply chain.

name: Green Mountain Spinnery
notes: Worker-owned cooperative in Putney, VT since 1981. Makes eco-friendly yarns from wool, alpaca, mohair, and organic cotton. All yarn spun in Vermont using traditional methods. One of the oldest worker co-ops in the US.

name: Odell Brewing Company
notes: 100% employee-owned (ESOP) since 2020. Founded 1989 in Fort Collins, CO. Known for 90 Shilling, IPA, and Rupture Flex IPA. Online shop sells hats, apparel, and accessories.

name: Deschutes Brewery
notes: 100% employee-owned (ESOP). Founded 1988 in Bend, OR. Known for Black Butte Porter and Mirror Pond Pale Ale. Sells beer and branded merch online.

name: Mast General Store
notes: Employee-owned (ESOP) since 1995. Historic general stores in NC, TN, VA, SC. Founded 1883, reopened 1980 by the Cooper family. Sells outdoor apparel, accessories, candy, toys, and Appalachian goods online.

name: Dean's Beans Organic Coffee
notes: 100% worker-owned cooperative since June 2023 after 30 years of founder ownership. Based in Orange, MA. Exclusively fair trade, organic, shade-grown coffees sourced from smallholder farmer cooperatives globally. Named Massachusetts Rural Business of the Year 2026.

name: Coraçao Chocolate
notes: Employee-owned (Certified EO), Certified Climate Neutral. Based in Richmond, CA. Handcrafted vegan, organic chocolates and confections. Makes truffles, bars, and seasonal gift boxes.

name: Harpoon Brewery
notes: 100% employee-owned (ESOP). Started 48% ESOP in 2014, completed full employee ownership in 2024. Founded 1986 in Boston, MA. Also brews in Windsor, VT. Known for UFO White and Leviathan Imperial Stout.

name: Maine Coast Sea Vegetables
notes: 100% employee-owned (ESOP) since 2017. Based in Hancock, ME. Harvests and processes wild Maine seaweeds: dulse, kelp, nori, wakame, Irish moss, sea lettuce, and blends. Also sells seaweed seasonings, teas, and supplements.

name: Sow True Seed
notes: Employee-owned (ESOP). Based in Asheville, NC. Sells heirloom vegetable, herb, flower, and cover crop seeds. Also sells gardening tools, books, and gifts. Certified by Certified EO.

name: The Local Butcher Shop
notes: Worker cooperative since 2021. Based in Berkeley, CA. Whole-animal butchery sourcing from small local farms. Online shop sells meat, charcuterie, sauces, and pantry items. Certified EO listed.

name: Trama Textiles
notes: Women-owned Maya weaving cooperative founded in 1988 in Guatemala. Worker-owned; all products handwoven by cooperative members. Sells traditional and contemporary woven clothing, scarves, bags, and home textiles. Fair trade and ethically made.

name: Barrio Brewing Co.
notes: 100% employee-owned (ESOP) since 2020. Based in Tucson, AZ. Founded 1991 by Dennis Arnold; one of Arizona's oldest craft breweries. Online shop sells branded merch — hats, glassware, shirts, anniversary items. WooCommerce store.

name: Tradehome Shoes
notes: 100% employee-owned (ESOP) since 2014. Based in Cottage Grove, MN. Family footwear retailer since 1921 with 100+ stores across the Midwest and South. Carries name-brand shoes for men, women, and kids. Certified EO listed.

name: Independent Fabrication
notes: Worker-owned cooperative since 1995. Based in Somerville, MA. Handmade steel and titanium custom road, gravel, and mountain bikes. One of the oldest worker-owned bicycle manufacturers in the US. Certified EO listed.

name: Stewart's Shops
notes: Employee-owned (ESOP) since 1974. Based in Saratoga Springs, NY. Iconic Upstate New York convenience store chain with 340+ locations. Online shop sells branded merchandise, private-label jams, coffee, and accessories. Employees own ~40% through ESOP.

name: Gardener's Supply Company
notes: Employee-owned (ESOP) since 1986. Based in Burlington, VT. One of the largest independent garden supply retailers in the US. Sells raised beds, planters, tools, seeds, composting equipment, and outdoor décor. Committed to sustainability and Vermont community.

name: Taharka Brothers Ice Cream
notes: Employee-owned (ESOP) since 2020. Based in Baltimore, MD. Handcrafted small-batch ice cream inspired by Baltimore culture and traditions. Known for flavors like Salted Honey Graham and Non-Dairy Chocolate Love. Named after a Nubian pharaoh. Mission-driven company employing returning citizens.

name: Applewood Seed Company
notes: Employee-owned (ESOP) since 2020. Based in Arvada, CO. One of America's leading wildflower seed companies since 1965. Sells native wildflower mixes, cover crop seed, bulk seed, and regional mixes. WooCommerce store.

name: Just Us! Coffee Roasters
notes: Canada's first certified organic and fair trade coffee roaster, founded 1995 as a worker co-op in Hortonville, Nova Scotia. Roasts to order. Known for social justice mission and direct trade with farming co-ops. 83 products including coffee, tea, and gift items.

name: Gimme! Coffee
notes: Worker co-op coffee roaster based in Ithaca, NY. Founded 2000, converted to worker co-op. Certified EO member. Roasts single-origin and blended coffees. Also sells brewing equipment and accessories.

name: Once Again Nut Butter
notes: Worker co-op based in Nunda, NY since 1976. Makes organic and natural peanut butter, almond butter, cashew butter, sunflower butter, honey, and tahini. B Corp certified. Certified EO member.

name: Dean's Beans Organic Coffee
notes: Worker co-op organic coffee roaster based in Orange, MA. Founded 1993 by Dean Cycon. Certified EO member. Known for social justice mission and direct relationships with farming communities. Ships coffee, tea, chocolate, and gift items.

name: Meow Meow Tweet
notes: Brooklyn NY worker collective making vegan, organic, zero-waste personal care products. Known for plastic-free packaging — compostable and recyclable. Products include deodorant, body oil, face care, and lip balm.

name: Alaffia
category: fair trade shea body care / hair care
notes: Worker co-op based in Olympia WA. Partners with women's shea butter co-ops in Togo, West Africa. Makes lotions, shampoos, conditioners, body washes from fair trade shea and African botanicals. B Corp certified.

name: Verso Books
notes: Worker co-op publishing house founded 1970 in London, with offices in Brooklyn NY. Largest independent radical publishing house in the English-speaking world. 750+ titles in print covering politics, history, philosophy, and culture.

name: PM Press
notes: Oakland CA worker co-op independent publisher founded 2007. Focuses on labor, anarchist, feminist, and social movement literature. Publishes books, e-books, DVDs, and music. Ships worldwide.

name: Resonate Cooperative
notes: Multi-stakeholder co-op music streaming platform owned by artists, listeners, and workers. Uses stream2own model — listeners eventually own the music they stream. Alternative to Spotify owned by its community.
